channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Unpatched Flaws Disclosed in Filesystem Bundled Into Millions of Embedded Devices
item.description:
Security firm runZero has&nbsp;disclosed seven vulnerabilities&nbsp;in&nbsp;FatFs, a small filesystem library that lets a device read and write the FAT and exFAT formats used on USB drives and SD cards.
The flaws matter because FatFs is nearly everywhere. It ships inside the firmware that runs security cameras, drones, industrial controllers, hardware crypto wallets, and other devices built on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android
item.description:
A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll (CVE-2026-46242) lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. It affects Linux desktops, servers, and Android, and a fix is out.
Bad Epoll sits in the same small stretch of kernel code where Anthropic's most powerful AI model, Mythos, recently found a different bug.
The AI caught one flaw and missed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New Avalon Malware Framework Packs CrownX Ransomware Capabilities
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented modular malware framework codenamed Avalon that's distributed by means of a multi-stage phishing chain capable of bypassing traditional security controls.
Avalon combines credential collection, lateral movement, remote access, recovery disruption, and ransomware execution, bringing together diverse functions under one
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: North Korea-Linked npm Packages Mimic Rollup Polyfills to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description:
Threat actors with ties to North Korea have been linked to a fresh set of malicious npm packages that masquerade as Rollup polyfill tooling to facilitate remote access and data theft.
According to JFrog, the packages "rollup-packages-polyfill-core" and "rollup-runtime-polyfill-core" mimic the legitimate "rollup-plugin-polyfill-node" project, down to the description, repository metadata, and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: In Other News: Canadian Hacker Jailed, Open Source Zero-Days, Two Sentenced for ATM Jackpotting
item.description: <p>Noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Anonymous-linked Canadian hacker jailed, researcher drops zero-days in open source projects, Venezuelans sentenced in the US over ATM jackpotting. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/in-other-news-canadian-hacker-jailed-open-source-zero-days-two-sentenced-for-atm-jackpotting/">In Other News: Canadian Hacker Jailed, Open Source Zero-Days, Two Sentenced for ATM Jackpotting</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: Armored Likho Targets Government Agencies, Power Sector with BusySnake Stealer
item.description:
A previously undocumented threat actor known as Armored Likho has been attributed to cyber attacks targeting government agencies and the electric power sector across Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan.
"Armored Likho blends financially motivated campaigns targeting private individuals with targeted cyber espionage aimed at organizations," Kaspersky said in a technical analysis published today. "
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chinese LLMs Broaden the Gap Between Attackers &amp; Defenders
item.description: Two new models from Chinese firms compete with top US mainstream and frontier models. Should cyber-defenders be worried?
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Qilin Dominates Ransomware Market Amid Growing Cybercrime Consolidation
item.description: The ransomware landscape is reconsolidating around major players, with Qilin emerging as the leading RaaS operation, researchers say

item.title: AI Agents Are Creating a New Enterprise Security Gap
item.description: <p>Five independent security disclosures in a single week point to the same gap: AI agent permissions, not AI agent capabilities, are the problem enterprises haven't solved.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-ai-agents-enterprise-security-gap/">AI Agents Are Creating a New Enterprise Security Gap</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Warning Over “Industrialized” Cyber-Attacks After Ransomware Gang Partners With TeamPCP
item.description: Researchers warn that collaboration could lead to “unprecedented” ransomware attacks, as FBI also issues warning

item.title: Flock Cameras Can Surveil Cars Without License Plates
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: European Parliament Member Investigating Spyware Was Hacked With Pegasus
item.description:
A new report from the Citizen Lab has revealed that former Member of the European Parliament Stelios Kouloglou had his mobile device repeatedly hacked with the notorious Pegasus spyware while serving on a committee that was tasked with investigating the abuse of such commercial surveillance tools in the bloc.
"Through forensic analysis of his device, we found that the attackers could have had
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Agentic AI Used to Conduct Ransomware Attack via Langflow
item.description: <p>Attack demonstrates how LLM agents can combine known exploitation techniques with real-time reasoning to automate complex, multi-stage intrusions.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/agentic-ai-used-to-conduct-ransomware-attack-via-langflow/">Agentic AI Used to Conduct Ransomware Attack via Langflow</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Medtronic Data Breach Impacts 3.8 Million People
item.description: <p>In April, ShinyHunters accessed the company’s corporate IT systems and stole patients’ personal and medical information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/medtronic-data-breach-impacts-3-8-million-people/">Medtronic Data Breach Impacts 3.8 Million People</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: FBI, Google Take Down NetNut Proxy Network Used by Cyber Threat Actors
item.description: The NetNut proxy network and the ‘Popa’ botnet are known to have infected devices with variants of Mirai DDoS botnets

item.title: Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker Extradited to US
item.description: <p>Prosecutors say 19-year-old Peter Stokes was a member of Scattered Spider, the hacking group linked to more than 100 network intrusions and over $100 million in ransom payments.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/alleged-scattered-spider-hacker-extradited-to-us/">Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker Extradited to US</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Higher Education TPRM in 2026: New Research Maps the Vendor Visibility Gap | UpGuard
item.description: Explore UpGuard’s latest research into higher education third-party risk, including supplier concentration and systemic vendor exposure.

item.title: Best TPRM Software for Higher Education: What to Look For | UpGuard
item.description: Learn how to evaluate higher education TPRM software, including HECVAT workflows, vendor monitoring, remediation, audit readiness, and reporting.

item.title: Google, FBI Disrupt NetNut Residential Proxy Network Powered by Millions of Devices
item.description: <p>NetNut rented access to millions of compromised devices, allowing cybercriminals and nation-state actors to mask their identities during attacks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/google-fbi-disrupt-netnut-residential-proxy-network-powered-by-millions-of-devices/">Google, FBI Disrupt NetNut Residential Proxy Network Powered by Millions of Devices</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that employs a series of clever tricks to infect systems and siphon sensitive data.
The stealer, discovered by Jamf Threat Labs, is distributed as a compiled AppleScript (.scpt) file impersonating Maccy, a legitimate open-source clipboard manager. It has been codenamed PamStealer owing to its ability to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical Cursor AI Code Editor Flaws Could Lead to OS-Level Remote Code Execution
item.description: <p>The DuneSlide vulnerabilities enable zero-click prompt injection attacks that escape Cursor's sandbox and execute arbitrary code on the underlying operating system.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/critical-cursor-ai-ide-flaws-could-lead-to-os-level-remote-code-execution/">Critical Cursor AI Code Editor Flaws Could Lead to OS-Level Remote Code Execution</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Aussies Face Reduced Cybercrime Risk, as Pressure Shifts to SMBs
item.description: Improved institutional safeguards and stricter regulations have pushed the burdens of protection and risk reduction on to Australian businesses.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Apple Reverses Age-Old Patch Policy to Keep Up With AI
item.description: Expect more compressed patching cycles from Apple going forward, as attackers leverage artificial intelligence to reduce time to exploit.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks after KrebsOnSecurity published findings from multiple security firms connecting NetNut to the Popa botnet, a collection of at least two million devices that have been compromised by malicious software with little or no consent from victims.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: FortiBleed Actors Collaborating With Inc, Lynx Ransomware Gangs
item.description: After gaining a foothold in thousands of Fortinet firewalls, the attackers are starting to monetize that access, and are also piling on a Nextcloud zero-day bug.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices
item.description:
Google has significantly degraded NetNut, one of the biggest networks that turns home devices into rented relays for other people's traffic.
Working with the FBI, Lumen, and others, Google's Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG)&nbsp;said this week&nbsp;it had reduced the network's pool of usable devices by millions.
Google identifies NetNut, also tracked as Popa, as a network spread across home
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
item.description:
Threat actors associated with the Anubis ransomware operation have been observed exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) vulnerability to obtain initial access.
"Although tactics differ between affiliates, common patterns emerged in tradecraft through use of legitimate Remote Management and Monitoring (RMM) tooling, credential access, and hands-on-keyboard procedures used for lateral
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Apple’s ‘Hide My Email’ Privacy Flaw Exposes Real Email Addresses
item.description: <p>Researchers say Apple’s Hide My Email flaw may expose real addresses, despite two fixes. Here’s what users should know about the privacy risk.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-apple-hide-my-email-privacy-flaw/">Apple’s ‘Hide My Email’ Privacy Flaw Exposes Real Email Addresses</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: AI Oversight, Security Flaws, and Industry Shifts Define This Week in Tech
item.description: <p>See what you missed in Daily Tech Insider from June 29–July 2.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/ai-oversight-security-flaws-and-industry-shifts-define-this-week-in-tech/">AI Oversight, Security Flaws, and Industry Shifts Define This Week in Tech</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Ransomware Thugs Masquerade as Interpol to Entice Small Biz
item.description: The ransomware campaign relies on basic social engineering and stretches across multiple regions, including the US, Europe, Middle East, and elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Detecting AI in the Software Supply Chain | UpGuard
item.description: The UpGuard Research team measured exposure to AI in the supply chain across 250 popular companies to understand this growing risk.

item.title: The User Risk Puzzle: Why Your Security Tools Don't Add Up | UpGuard
item.description: Struggling to manage user risk at your organization? Discover how to unify fragmented security data for a clear, cohesive view and stronger defense.

item.title: What is Shadow SaaS? Causes, Risks, and Management Tips | UpGuard
item.description: Discover what shadow SaaS is, the risks it poses, and how to detect, manage, and integrate it into your third-party risk management strategy.

item.title: Asana Discloses Data Exposure Bug in MCP Server | UpGuard
item.description: Asana identified a bug in its Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that may have exposed data to MCP users in other Asana accounts.

item.title: Solving Human Risk: Close the Visibility Gap | UpGuard
item.description: Don't fly blind. In this blog, we expose the full scope of human risk visibility challenges and explore how User Risk provides the tools to address them.

item.title: Downstream Data: Investigating AI Data Leaks in Flowise | UpGuard
item.description: A thousand Flowise instances are exposed to the internet, many of them leaking confidential business data, passwords, and more.

item.title: Uncovering the Shadow AI Paradox | UpGuard
item.description: Our research into the motives of shadow AI user led to surprising discoveries.

item.title: Solving Human Risk: Automate Governance and Prioritize Action | UpGuard
item.description: Visibility is not enough. Discover how User Risk empowers automated governance and drives immediate action by prioritizing human risk data.

item.title: New Chrome Update Fixes 382 Security Bugs Across Desktop, Mobile
item.description: <p>Google released a Chrome update addressing 382 security bugs, including sandbox-escape risks. Users and IT teams should update quickly.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-chrome-update-382-security-bugs/">New Chrome Update Fixes 382 Security Bugs Across Desktop, Mobile</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Solving Human Risk: Build a Measurable, Security-First Culture | UpGuard
item.description: Turn your workforce into your first line of defense. Learn how User Risk builds a measurable, security-first culture and secures your human layer.

item.title: Cybersecurity Predictions for 2026: Human Risk, AI Data Leaks, and the Next Big Breach | UpGuard
item.description: Get one step ahead of the next big shock. Four security predictions for 2026—likely, uncomfortable, and easy to miss.

item.title: Human Factors in Cybersecurity in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Discover the primary human factors that could be disrupting the efficacy of your cybersecurity program and learn how to mitigate them.

item.title: Productivity at a Price: The Rising Cost of AI Convenience | UpGuard
item.description: AI tools offer efficiency, but at a cost. UpGuard reveals how PII leaks and new supply chain vulnerabilities are hidden in widely used AI workflows.

item.title: The Shadow AI Data Leak Problem No One's Talking About | UpGuard
item.description: Is your team unknowingly leaking sensitive data through its AI usage? Discover the dangers of Shadow AI and strategies for secure AI adoption at work.

item.title: Emerging Risks: Typosquatting in the MCP Ecosystem | UpGuard
item.description: The MCP server ecosystem is susceptible to brand impersonation for both local and remote servers.

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: The SSO & AI Visibility Gap | UpGuard
item.description: Is your SSO enough? 31.4% of vendor interactions bypass identity logs. Discover how Shadow AI and direct logins redefine vendor visibility.

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: A Pivot To Usage-Based Discovery | UpGuard
item.description: Pivot from "purchase-based" to usage-based discovery. See how browser telemetry and identity logs work together to secure your true vendor footprint.

item.title: The Shadow Supply Chain: A 72.9% Gap In Your Vendor Inventory | UpGuard
item.description: Your real vendor inventory is 8x larger than your official list. Discover how user behavior creates a hidden shadow supply chain that legacy tools miss.

item.title: ThreatsDay: AI Compute Hijacking, Apple Email Flaw, BlueHammer Ransomware + 14 Stories
item.description:
This week’s security news is mostly about weak spots.
Browsers, bots, sandboxes, AI systems, and email flows all show the same problem in different ways. Everything looks normal until someone tests a small gap and finds a way through.
This is not one big break. It is small permissions, weak checks, open systems, and normal tools doing things they were allowed to do. That same pattern runs
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New CitrixBleed Vulnerability Exploited Immediately After Public Disclosure
item.description: <p>Hackers are targeting NetScaler appliances using public PoC code to retrieve arbitrary memory content in the HTTP response.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/new-citrixbleed-vulnerability-exploited-immediately-after-public-disclosure/">New CitrixBleed Vulnerability Exploited Immediately After Public Disclosure</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Why a Windows Hello PIN Beats a Password for Enterprise Security
item.description: <p>As phishing campaigns, AI-driven identity attacks, and Windows migration planning raise authentication stakes, IT teams should recheck how Windows Hello PIN security works.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-hello-pin-security/">Why a Windows Hello PIN Beats a Password for Enterprise Security</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: How to Conduct a Successful Audit of AI-Driven Software Development
item.description: <p>As AI-generated code becomes commonplace, CISOs need new audit strategies to measure developer practices, govern AI tool usage, and identify software risks before they reach production.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/how-to-conduct-a-successful-audit-of-ai-driven-software-development/">How to Conduct a Successful Audit of AI-Driven Software Development</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Matias Madou

item.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
item.description:
The threat actor known as ToddyCat has been attributed to a new malware called Umbrij that's designed to gain surreptitious access to a victim's email correspondence via the Google API.
"In this campaign, the attackers focused their attention on corporate email communications hosted on Gmail, targeting access compromise via APIs," Kaspersky said in a detailed report published this week. "
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Best Threat Intelligence Platforms and Vendors | UpGuard
item.description: Compare the best threat intelligence platforms (TIPs) and vendors of 2026. Learn key features, criteria, and find the perfect tool for your security team.

item.title: Researcher Behind 'Exploitarium' Explains Release of Undisclosed Zero-Day Exploits
item.description: Infosecurity spoke with the researcher who dumped over 30 proof-of-concept exploits without disclosing the vulnerabilities first

item.title: FortiBleed Campaign Linked to INC, Lynx Ransomware Attacks
item.description: <p>Researchers say credentials harvested from hundreds of thousands of FortiGate firewalls are being used to facilitate ransomware attacks by the INC and Lynx operations.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/fortibleed-campaign-linked-to-inc-lynx-ransomware-attacks/">FortiBleed Campaign Linked to INC, Lynx Ransomware Attacks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Anthropic's AI Finds Bugs. IBM Bets $5B It Can Fix Them.
item.description: IBM and Red Hat assign 20,000 engineers to the new Project Lightwell service as Anthropic's Mythos findings ignite debate over how to secure the open-source software supply chain.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: Cybercriminals Pose as Interpol in Phishing Emails to Infect Victims With Ransomware
item.description: Bitdefender researchers warned of curious ransomware campaign which has targeted businesses around the world

item.title: Microsoft Flags MCP Tool Descriptions as Hidden AI Agent Attack Path
item.description: <p>Microsoft warns that poisoned MCP tool descriptions can steer AI agents into leaking sensitive data through approved tool calls.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-mcp-tool-risk/">Microsoft Flags MCP Tool Descriptions as Hidden AI Agent Attack Path</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
item.description: Identity lifecycle management was architected around a person with an employment record, a manager, and a departure date. AI agents have none of those. As autonomous principals proliferate across enterprise environments, the governance model built for humans develops structural blind spots that traditional IGA tools weren't designed to detect. This guide covers where that model breaks, what it
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Cybersecurity Mission Creep in the US
item.description: <p>Interesting paper: &#8220;<a href="https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4588977">Cybersecurity Mission Creep</a>.&#8221;</p> <blockquote><p><b>Abstract:</b> Cybersecurity is experiencing mission creep. Policymakers are casting more and more problems as issues of cybersecurity. So reframed, wildly different policy issues, from misinformation, to child social media safety laws, to antitrust regulations, to alleged journalist misconduct, to anti-sex trafficking statutes become what this Article calls &#8220;cybersecuritized.&#8221; Before this reframing, these issues present as important but not existential. But once cybersecuritization positions the issues as threats intensified by their technological nature, they gain access to the politics and law of urgency and exceptionalism and invite troubling governance responses...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Claude Models After Cybersecurity Alarm
item.description: <p>Anthropic said Tuesday night that its AI model called Claude Fable 5 is now widely available.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/trump-administration-lifts-restrictions-on-anthropics-claude-models-after-cybersecurity-alarm/">Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Claude Models After Cybersecurity Alarm</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: Best Vulnerability Management Tools & Software in 2026 | UpGuard
item.description: Discover the best vulnerability management tools on the market and learn which vulnerability detection solution is best for your organization.

item.title: NCSC Shares Tips on How to Make a Pen Tester’s Job Harder
item.description: The NCSC has shared best practice advice from pen testers which could help improve system resilience

item.title: AI Agent Exploits Langflow RCE to Automate Database Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Security firm&nbsp;Sysdig&nbsp;says it has found what it believes is the first ransomware attack run from start to finish by an AI agent.
Its Threat Research Team calls the operator JADEPUFFER and says a large language model handled the whole job: breaking in, stealing credentials, moving deeper into the network, then encrypting and wiping a company's production database.
Ransomware has always
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Alleged Scattered Spider Member Extradited to US
item.description: A teenager accused of hacking as part of Scattered Spider has been arrested

item.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
item.description:
The recently discovered financially-motivated FortiBleed campaign has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations, indicating that the verified, stolen credentials were intended for follow-on intrusions.
"An operator tied to FortiBleed's infrastructure was found actively working negotiation panels for both groups, tying mass FortiGate credential theft directly to ransomware deployment
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: New ChocoPoC RAT Targets Vulnerability Researchers via Fake PoC Exploit Repos
item.description:
Attackers are hiding a data-stealing trojan inside fake exploit code aimed at the people who hunt bugs for a living. The malware, called ChocoPoC, travels in Python proof-of-concept (PoC) repositories on GitHub that claim to exploit hot new CVEs.
Run one, and it quietly lifts your saved passwords, browser cookies, and files, then hands the attacker a shell on your machine.&nbsp;YesWeHack and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-45659 Added to CISA KEV After Active Exploitation
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a high-severity flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45659 (CVSS score: 8.8), is a case of remote code execution arising from the deserialization of untrusted data. The issue
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Best Data Breach & Data Leak Detection Tools | UpGuard
item.description: Stopping an intrusion is half the battle. This guide ranks the top breach and leak detection tools to neutralize exposed credentials before attackers do.

item.title: Microsoft Warns: Fake Perplexity Extension Abused Chrome Search Features
item.description: <p>Microsoft found a fake Perplexity AI Chrome extension that rerouted searches through attacker servers. Here’s what users should check now.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fake-perplexity-chrome-extension-searches/">Microsoft Warns: Fake Perplexity Extension Abused Chrome Search Features</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Crafty Phishing Campaigns Auto-Adapt to Victim's Device, OS
item.description: Attackers fingerprint victims through user-agent data to deliver OS-specific payloads, increasing compromise rates and campaign profitability.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: And the Winner in Dominant Malware Delivery? ClickFix
item.description: Researchers say the highly effective social engineering technique is no longer the exception for malware attacks — it's now the rule.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Unpatched Argo CD Repo-Server Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Kubernetes Clusters
item.description:
Argo CD, a widely used tool for deploying software to Kubernetes, has an unpatched flaw in its repo-server component that lets an unauthenticated attacker run code, provided they can reach the component's internal network port.
Synacktiv, which found the bug, says it can lead to a full cluster takeover. There is no fix and no CVE. The firm says it reported the flaw to Argo CD's maintainers in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
item.description:
A teenager accused of belonging to the hacking group Scattered Spider has been extradited from Finland to face U.S. charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud, the U.S. Department of Justice&nbsp;announced&nbsp;on July 1.
Peter Stokes, 19, a dual U.S. and Estonian citizen, appeared in a Chicago federal court on June 30, where a judge ordered him held in custody.
Finnish police
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Aflac Data Breach: Over 4M Customers in Japan May Be at Risk
item.description: <p>Aflac says a data breach in Japan may affect 4.38 million customers and agents, exposing personal, policy, and some banking information.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-aflac-japan-data-breach-insurance-records/">Aflac Data Breach: Over 4M Customers in Japan May Be at Risk</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Microsoft Uncovers Widespread Hotel Phishing Campaign in Japan
item.description: <p>Microsoft and Trend Micro found hotel phishing attacks using fake guest complaints and photo links to target staff in Japan.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-hotel-phishing-apac-japan/">Microsoft Uncovers Widespread Hotel Phishing Campaign in Japan</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: SEO-Poisoned Software Sites Abuse ScreenConnect to Deploy AsyncRAT
item.description:
Unknown threat actors are leveraging the ScreenConnect remote access tool as a way to deploy and execute AsyncRAT.
Kaspersky said the activity is part of a "massive, multi-domain, multi-language" campaign that distributes malicious installer archives hosted on spoofed websites.
These installers masquerade as popular software like OBS Studio, DNS Jumper, DS4Windows, and Bandicam, among others.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: VEIL#DROP Malware Chain Uses Blogger Platform to Deliver PureLogs Stealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new multi-stage malware delivery attack chain that uses social engineering and Blogger pages to deliver an information stealer called PureLogs.
The activity has been codenamed VEIL#DROP by Securonix. It's suspected that the initial payloads are distributed either via spear-phishing or a drive-by compromise, which occurs when an unsuspecting user lands on
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Shadow IT: Tiering the Unseen to Manage Vendor Risk | UpGuard
item.description: Invisible apps become riskier with time. Use real-time user signals to categorize every vendor in your ecosystem based on its true risk profile.

item.title: MCP: The AI Protocol Quietly Expanding Your Attack Surface | UpGuard
item.description: As AI tools adopt MCP, security teams face a new shadow IT crisis. Learn what MCP is, why these servers carry risk, and how to begin gaining visibility.

item.title: Six MCP Security Incidents Every Security Leader Should Know | UpGuard
item.description: From registry poisoning to filesystem wipes: discover the 6 MCP security incidents every leader must know to secure their AI agent workflows in 2026.

item.title: Shadow MCP Servers: The AI Infrastructure You Can't See | UpGuard
item.description: In 2012, it was Dropbox. In 2026, it’s Shadow MCP. Discover why unvetted AI agents are an invisible threat and how to regain total visibility.

item.title: Practical MCP Security: A Playbook for Mid-Market Teams | UpGuard
item.description: Move from zero to a defensible MCP security baseline in 90 days. Get a practical, phased playbook for lean teams to secure agentic AI infrastructure.

item.title: When Too Much Security Data Became the Risk
item.description: Rapid growth turned routine firewall logs into a security and budget liability. One CISO used artificial intelligence to filter what data truly belongs in the SIEM.
item.dc:creator: Joan Goodchild

item.title: New Cargo Theft Surge: From Lobster Heists To Bourbon Warehouse Scams
Ousaban Banking Trojan Targets Iberian Bank Users with Fake PDF Lures
item.description:
A Brazilian banking trojan called Ousaban is going after Windows users who bank in Spain and Portugal.&nbsp;Fortinet's FortiGuard Labs&nbsp;identified the campaign in May 2026.
It opens with a phishing PDF disguised as a corrupted file, checks that the visitor is really in Spain or Portugal, and hides its real payload inside an image.
The goal is the usual one: steal banking logins and take
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Adobe Patches 7 CVSS 10.0 Flaws in ColdFusion and Campaign Classic
item.description:
Adobe has released patches for multiple maximum-severity security flaws impacting Adobe ColdFusion and Adobe Campaign Classic.
The ColdFusion updates "resolves critical and important vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, arbitrary file system read, and security feature bypass," Adobe said in an alert released Tuesday.
The vulnerabilities are listed
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 'Phantom Squatting': An Emerging AI-Driven Supply Chain Threat
item.description: LLMs consistently hallucinate Web domains for legitimate brands that attackers can register for malicious activity in a difficult-to-detect attack vector.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Critical Cursor Flaws Could Let Prompt Injection Escape Sandbox and Run Commands
item.description:
Two flaws in Cursor, an AI code editor, could let a single, ordinary-looking prompt break out of the editor's safety sandbox and run any command on a developer's computer. There is no click to fall for and no approval box to ignore.
Cato AI Labs&nbsp;found the pair and named them DuneSlide. They are tracked as&nbsp;CVE-2026-50548&nbsp;and&nbsp;CVE-2026-50549, both rated 9.8 out of 10 (or 9.3
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Fileless Malware Abuses Google Blogspot to Deploy Infostealer in Memory
item.description: Securonix said the Veil#Drop campaign abuses Google Blogspot to deliver PureLog Stealer in memory

item.title: Free Windows 10 Security Updates Run Until 2027, but Not Every PC Qualifies
item.description: <p>Microsoft’s consumer ESU program includes a no-cost enrollment option for eligible Windows 10 PCs, but managed devices and some account setups are excluded.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-windows-10-esu-eligibility/">Free Windows 10 Security Updates Run Until 2027, but Not Every PC Qualifies</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Pre-Auth RCE Flaw Faces Active Exploitation Attempts
item.description:
A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Progress Kemp LoadMaster is seeing active exploitation attempts, according to an advisory from eSentire's Threat Response Unit (TRU).
The Canadian cybersecurity company said it identified exploitation attempts targeting CVE-2026-8037 (CVSS score: 9.6), an operating system (OS) command injection flaw that could be exploited to achieve
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Brazilian Banking Trojan Ousaban Targets Spain and Portugal
item.description: FortiGuard says the Brazilian banking trojan Ousaban is targeting Spain and Portugal via phishing

item.title: Safe Events Start With Threat Intel &amp; Digital Security
item.description: Planning ahead to defend against cyber threats is the work that keeps events uneventful.
item.dc:creator: Olga Polishchuk

item.title: AI-Generated Browser Ransomware Abuses Chromium API on Windows, Linux, macOS, Android
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware artifact generated using DeepSeek that constructed a novel attack path combining "unrealistic browser-malware concepts with a real browser capability" to turn it into a working ransomware technique that runs entirely inside the browser on both Windows and Android devices.
"This is the first documented case where a frontier AI model
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Edge AI History Search Raises New Privacy Questions for IT Teams
item.description: <p>Microsoft Edge 150 keeps AI history search policy in play, giving IT teams a reason to audit browser AI controls, Copilot access, search suggestions, and extensions.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-edge-ai-history-privacy/">Microsoft Edge AI History Search Raises New Privacy Questions for IT Teams</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: 2026 Cybersecurity Assessment: The Gap Between Awareness and Resilience
item.description:
Organizations have never had greater awareness of cyber risk. Yet turning that awareness into operational resilience has never been more challenging. The 2026 Bitdefender Cybersecurity Assessment confirms this is the case, as this year's findings reveal a series of surprising contradictions.
Here are a few examples, based on the independent survey of 1,200 IT and cybersecurity professionals
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Are Back with New Security Guardrails
item.description: The new classifier in Fable 5 blocks the jailbreak technique that prompted the US export controls “in over 99% of cases”

item.title: Papa Johns Surveillance-Based Advertising
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier
